title.rendered: Vatican Cellars: Darkness on the Edge of Town

content.rendered:
If I had less restraint, I’d probably feature a cover from the outstanding Play Some Pool, Skip Some School, Act Real Cool compilation (released back in 2009) at least once a week. There are so many great tracks on that album–I really can’t recommend it enough.

Today, the spotlight is on one of my favorites: a gorgeous cello and double bass-led arrangement of “Darkness on the Edge of Town” by Vatican Cellars.

Vatican Cellars is a five-piece “folk noir” band from the UK, and that’s a pretty apt description.

Their subject matter is dark, and their vocals are somber. But oh, those strings… I just lose myself in that bridge every time.

Take a listen and enjoy (and then trust me and get the compilation).

© August 6, 2020

excerpt.rendered: Back in 2009, Vatican Cellars gave us a cover of “Darkness on the Edge of Town” both daring and beautiful–check out their cello-led arrangement inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: The Winter Blanket: Darkness on the Edge of Town
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The Winter Blanket gives us a quiet, introspective take of "Darkness on the Edge of Town" that conveys the resignation and defeat lurking at the heart of the song.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Gresham Flyers: Magic
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: From the outstanding tribute album Play Some Pool, Skip Some School, Act Real Cool comes this seriously sinister and rocking cover of Bruce's moody original.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Introduction
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce Springsteen's fourth studio album becomes the first to receive the complete Roll of the Dice treatment.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Drive-By Truckers: Adam Raised a Cain
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Alt-country band Drive-by Truckers proves their rock bona fides whenever they cover this classic from Darkness on the Edge of Town.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Darkness on the Edge of Town
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Darkness on the Edge of Town" is one of Bruce's most enduring classics. But is it a song about heroic defiance or defeat and addiction?

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 13
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce joins Paul Simon and an all-star line-up to combat homelessness, the E Street Band plays the entire Darkness album to an empty theater, and more.
